name: ARTERIOVENOUS FISTULA
definition: AVF abnormal communication between artery + vein resulting in tremendous amount of flow due to abnormal communication between veins of cavernous sinus and ≥ 1 branches of internal / often high-flow lesions with direct fistulous communication between a pial artery + a vein

demographics.Location: cavernous sinus (20-40%), transverse / sigmoid sinus (20-60%), tentorium (12-

distribution: cavernous sinus (20-40%), transverse / sigmoid sinus (20-60%), tentorium (12-
discriminating_features: NO nidus / classic intraparenchymal tangle of vessels
± visualization of feeding dural branches of ECA / ICA
dilatation + tortuosity of ipsilateral superior ophthalmic vein, facial veins, internal jugular
enlargement of dural venous sinuses ← increased venous flow + pressure

general: ± visualization of feeding dural branches of ECA / ICA
dilatation + tortuosity of ipsilateral superior ophthalmic vein, facial veins, internal jugular
enlargement of dural venous sinuses ← increased venous flow + pressure
focal asymmetric / diffuse enlargement of cavernous sinus
enlargement of superior orbital fissure (in chronic phase)
US: arterial flow in cavernous sinus + superior ophthalmic vein
CECT: multiple small vessels within wall of thrombosed / partially recanalized stenotic dural
dilated transcalvarial channels ← transosseous feeding artery
MR: NO true nidus within brain parenchyma
dilated vessels of brain surface (from MCA, ACA, PCA)
asymmetric dilatation of pial feeder artery
± dilated venous pouches outside brain parenchyma
NO nidus / classic intraparenchymal tangle of vessels
ANGIO: ipsilateral ICA contrast injection shows wall of ICA to be incomplete
contralateral ICA contrast injection + compression of involved ICA
early opacification of veins of cavernous sinus
retrograde flow through dilated superior ophthalmic vein

clinical.Rx: transvenous / transarterial coil ablation ± stent placement; latex / silicone balloon
